name: Behind-the-Back Dumbbell Shrug
description: Stand with dumbbells held behind the body (palms facing back, arms straight). The behind-the-back position biases lower trap and mid trap recruitment versus the standard front-shrug variant which is more upper-trap dominant. Shrug straight up — squeeze the shoulder blades DOWN and BACK at the top, not just elevation. 2-3s hold at the top, slow controlled eccentric. Don't roll shoulders. Keep ribs down throughout. Common cue: 'pull the shoulder blades into your back pockets at the top, not up to your ears.'
